summary_text:
S:
The patient reports ongoing management of psoriatic arthritis and intermittent joint pain, particularly in her feet. She reports taking an extra dose of prednisone occasionally due to increased pain on days with more activity, such as when she's off work. She also mentions experiencing bruising easily, even from minor bumps, and reports receiving a letter regarding a potential interference with the efficacy of the COVID vaccine due to methotrexate. The patient reports feeling horrible after receiving the COVID vaccine and taking the second dose to alleviate the pain.

O:
Physical exam reveals good range of motion in the toes and visible nail ridges consistent with psoriasis. The patient's blood pressure is within normal limits. Neurological exam is unremarkable.

A:
The patient's primary problem is psoriatic arthritis, complicated by potential medication side effects and a recent adverse reaction to the COVID vaccine. The bruising is likely related to prednisone.

P:
We will discontinue the prednisone and continue with the methotrexate. I will order blood work to monitor the methotrexate and assess for potential side effects. I will schedule a follow-up appointment in three months to assess treatment progress and address any ongoing concerns. I will also provide instructions to monitor for bruising and report any worsening symptoms.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Psoriatic arthritis management.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Judy Gomez is a 61-year-old female who presents to the clinic today for ongoing management of psoriatic arthritis.

Ms. Gomez is currently taking methotrexate and prednisone 1 mg daily. She believes methotrexate has been relieving her joint pain.

The patient reports she has been doing well since her last visit. She has been able to decrease her prednisone dose to 1 mg daily; however, she took 2 mg for a couple of days due to increased pain in her bilateral feet.

The patient states when she received her first COVID-19 vaccine she held her methotrexate and felt "horrible" all week until the next Wednesday when she took it. She felt better by the end of the week. She did not hold methotrexate for her second COVID-19 vaccine.

PHYSICAL EXAM

Musculoskeletal: Full range of motion. Dystrophy of all the nails of the toes.

ASSESSMENT

• Psoriatic arthritis.
• High risk medication use.

PLAN

Psoriatic arthritis.
Stable on methotrexate and prednisone 1 mg daily. The patient will discontinue prednisone and continue methotrexate.

INSTRUCTIONS

The patient will follow up in 3 months.
